highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.162 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a `waitingFor` field to `claude agents --json` output, so you can see exactly what a paused session is blocked on (like a permission prompt). It also fixes a long-standing issue where explicitly listing Grep/Glob in `--tools` was silently ignored on native builds — those dedicated search tools now actually activate. If you use multi-agent workflows or custom tool configurations, upgrade now.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.160 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds important safety prompts before Claude writes to shell startup files like `.zshenv` or `.bash_login`, preventing accidental command execution changes to your environment. The `acceptEdits` mode now also pauses before touching build-tool config files like `.npmrc` that could silently grant code execution rights. Developers using automated or headless workflows should review how these new prompts affect their pipelines.

highlights.title: Claude Code – Everything you can configure that the docs don't tell you
highlights.summary: A developer dug into the Claude Code source code and surfaced undocumented configuration options that can significantly change how the tool behaves. This is a goldmine for power users who feel constrained by the official docs and want finer control over Claude Code's behavior. Bookmark this if you want to go beyond the defaults and tailor Claude Code to your workflow.

highlights.title: Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code
highlights.summary: Anthropic's official blog post introduces dynamic workflows, a feature that lets Claude Code adapt its task execution plan on the fly rather than following a rigid script. This is especially useful for complex, multi-step coding tasks where conditions change mid-execution. Developers building agentic pipelines should read this to understand how to leverage more flexible, responsive automation.

highlights.title: Python utility package for building Claude Code hooks
highlights.summary: This open-source Python package (`claude-hook-utils`) provides ready-made utilities to make writing Claude Code hooks much easier and less boilerplate-heavy. Hooks let you intercept and customize Claude Code's behavior at key lifecycle points, and this library abstracts the tricky parts. If you want to extend Claude Code with custom logic — like logging, guardrails, or notifications — this is a great starting point.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: A concise YouTube tutorial aimed at getting developers productive with Claude Code quickly, covering the essentials in a tightly-scoped 30-minute format. This is a great pick if you've installed Claude Code but aren't sure how to make the most of it day-to-day. Watch it during a lunch break and come back to your project with concrete techniques to try immediately.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.8, the latest and most capable model in the Opus 4 family. This release is directly relevant to Claude Code users because Auto mode for Opus 4.8 is now available on Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry — meaning enterprise teams can access it through their existing cloud provider. Enable it by setting the `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1` environment variable.

highlights.title: Anthropic's Full History: From OpenAI Defectors to a Nearly Trillion-Dollar AI Empire (Chinese)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video (original title: 'Anthropic全史：从OpenAI叛逃者，到估值万亿的AI帝国') covers the five-year history of Anthropic, from its founding by ex-OpenAI researchers to its recent $965 billion valuation. It frames the company's rise through the lens of capital, compute, and geopolitical competition, and raises the question of whether idealistic founders eventually become what they set out to fight. Useful context for developers who want to understand the company behind Claude Code and where it might be headed.

tips_of_the_day: Run `claude agents --json` and pipe the output to `jq '.[] | select(.waitingFor != null)'` to instantly spot any stalled agent sessions blocked on permission prompts — great for debugging headless or CI workflows.

Before upgrading to a new Claude Code release, check the changelog for any new prompts added to `acceptEdits` mode — sometimes a release adds interactive confirmation steps that can break scripts expecting fully automated edits.

Set `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1` in your `.env` or shell profile when using Claude Code via AWS Bedrock or Google Vertex so you get the smarter, model-selected context window behavior without changing any other config.
